issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44 px recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 36 px, which is below the WCAG minimum touch target size of 44 × 44 px. Padding on the vertical axis is only 6 px per side, also below the 8 px recommendation, so the effective target may be too small for finger users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector defines a transition that animates the transform property, and the :hover state applies a translateY(-1px) transform. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion) rule disables this motion, causing unwanted animation for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 420: .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); }
  Line 440: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in the footer remove the outline and do not supply an alternative focus style.
issuesFound.fix: Line 970: `.footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social‑media links hide the focus outline without providing a visible replacement, hindering keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 990: `.social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44 px recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` has a `min-height: 32px` and vertical padding of ~7.5 px, resulting in an effective touch area smaller than the recommended 44 × 44 px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: `.btn { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm (height 20 px)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-sm` sets `min-height: 20px` with insufficient padding, creating a touch target smaller than the WCAG minimum of 24 px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon (20 px × 20 px)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-icon` defines `min-width: 20px; min-height: 20px` with no extra padding, violating the 24 px minimum touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360: `.btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-2); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44 px recommended size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: `.nav-toggle` has `min-height: 28px; min-width: 28px` and only 6 px vertical padding, resulting in a touch area smaller than the recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: `.nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-3); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44 px recommended size on .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons in the pricing toggle have `min-height: 28px` with 6 px vertical padding, producing a touch target under 44 px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: `.pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44 px recommended size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons use `min-height: 28px` and 6 px vertical padding, resulting in a sub‑minimum touch area.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: `.filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which can cause words to run together and reduce readability for all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 120: `body { word-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` animates `transform` on hover, but no reduced‑motion override disables this for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Add inside the existing reduced‑motion block:  
  Line 860: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of `.hero-image img` applies a 3‑D transform, which is not suppressed for reduced‑motion users.
issuesFound.fix:
Add inside the reduced‑motion block:  
  Line 862: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (translateY)
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button moves upward on hover via `transform: translateY(-1px)`, with no reduced‑motion fallback.
issuesFound.fix:
Add inside the reduced‑motion block:  
  Line 864: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global `a` selector removes `text-decoration`, eliminating the default underline without providing another visual cue (e.g., color change, border). Users may not be able to distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 110: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` (or add a non‑color visual differentiator such as `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;`).

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription form validates the email address on submit, but when the email is invalid it only displays a text error message and does not set `aria‑invalid="true"` on the `<input type="email">`. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden from AT when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The off‑canvas mobile navigation drawer (`_drawer`) is shown/hidden by toggling CSS classes (`is‑open`) but never receives `aria‑hidden="true"` when it is closed. Screen readers can still navigate into the hidden drawer, causing a focus trap and confusing experience.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened or closed, the code updates `aria‑expanded` on the trigger but does not provide an audible announcement of the state change. Users of assistive technologies receive no feedback that the menu has opened.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing period toggle (`_toggleEl`) is a custom switch that toggles between monthly and annual billing. The code sets `aria‑checked` on the element but does not set `aria‑pressed`, which is required for a button that represents a two‑state toggle.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update without dedicated live region (search result count)
issuesFound.explanation: After applying a filter in the product catalogue, the visible product count is updated in the UI (`_updateCountBadge`) but no dedicated live region is used to announce the new count. Users of screen readers are not informed that the number of results has changed.

fpTitles: Form validation error not reflected in aria‑invalid
Mobile navigation drawer hidden from AT when closed
Dropdown menu open/close not announced to screen readers
Toggle button missing aria‑pressed
Dynamic content update without dedicated live region (search result count)

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the filter is applied the visible count badge is updated (`_countBadge.textContent = String(count);`) but no ARIA live region is used to announce the new total to screen‑reader users, leaving them unaware of how many products match.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden element with `role="status"` / `aria-live="polite"` (or use the existing live region) and set its text to “{count} products shown” after updating the badge.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter empty‑state message not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When no products match the current filters the element `[data-empty-state]` is shown, but there is no live‑region announcement, so users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the result set is empty.
issuesFound.fix: Insert an element with `role="alert"` or `aria-live="assertive"` inside the empty‑state container and update its text (e.g., “No products match the selected filters”) when it becomes visible.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter pagination changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pagination text (“Page X of Y”) is updated in the DOM but never announced, so users cannot know that the page of results has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Add an `aria-live="polite"` element (or reuse the live region) and set its content to the same pagination string each time the page changes.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter sorting changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the sort order updates the UI but does not provide an audible cue; screen‑reader users are not informed that the list has been re‑sorted.
issuesFound.fix: After applying the new sort, call `liveRegion.announce('Products sorted by ' + sortLabel)` (where `sortLabel` is a human‑readable description of the selected sort).

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter active‑filter chips updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Adding or removing filter chips updates the visual list but no live‑region notification is emitted, so users cannot know which filters are currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering the chips, update a hidden live region with a summary such as “Filters applied: Category ‘X’, Tag ‘Y’, Search ‘Z’”.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle lacks `aria-pressed` state
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons (`data-view-mode`) toggle between grid and list but only set `aria-pressed` on the clicked button; the other button’s state is not explicitly set to `false`, which can leave the opposite button in an indeterminate state for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, set `setAttr(otherButton, 'aria-pressed', 'false')` for the non‑active button.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle does not have an associated `aria-controls` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons control the visibility/layout of the product grid but do not reference the grid via `aria-controls`, missing an explicit relationship for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(button, 'aria-controls', <grid‑id>)` where `<grid‑id>` is the `id` of the `[data-product-grid]` element.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter pagination element lacks `role="navigation"` and `aria-label`
issuesFound.explanation: The pagination text is inserted into a generic container without semantic navigation role, making it harder for assistive technology to convey its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Wrap the pagination text in an element with `role="navigation"` and `aria-label="Product list pagination"`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter sorting `<select>` does not have an associated label (`aria-label` or `<label>`)
issuesFound.explanation: The `<select>` used for sorting is identified only by `data-sort-key`; without a visible label or `aria-label`, screen‑reader users cannot discover its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_sortSelect, 'aria-label', 'Sort products by')` or ensure a `<label for="…">` element is present in the markup.

issuesFound.title: FAQ panel missing `aria‑hidden` when opened
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item, …)` function in the FAQ accordion adds the `open` state and sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger, but it never removes `aria‑hidden` from the answer panel, leaving the panel hidden from assistive technologies even when visible.

issuesFound.title: FAQ panel missing `aria‑hidden` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `close(item, …)` function sets `aria‑expanded="false"` on the trigger and hides the panel with `hidden`, but it does not add `aria‑hidden="true"` to the panel, so the panel may remain exposed to AT.

issuesFound.title: FAQ triggers lack initial `aria‑expanded` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqRegistry.parse()` the trigger button receives `aria-controls` and other ARIA attributes, but no `aria-expanded` is set on page load. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the panel is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
  const isInitiallyOpen = …;
  setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(isInitiallyOpen)); // <-- add this line
  ```

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels missing `aria‑hidden` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance()` in the dropdown menu sets `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger and removes the `open` class, but it never adds `aria‑hidden="true"` to the panel, leaving hidden menus exposed to AT.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search status updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `siteSearch._updateStatus()` writes status text into `_statusEl` but the element lacks `aria-live="polite"` or `assertive`, so users of assistive technology receive no audible feedback when a search succeeds, fails, or returns no results.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
  function _updateStatus(count, isError) {
    …
    _statusEl.setAttribute('aria-live', isError ? 'assertive' : 'polite'); // <-- add this line
    …
  }
  ```

issuesFound.title: Site‑search does not announce the number of suggestions returned
issuesFound.explanation: After fetching suggestions, `_fetchAndRender()` renders the list but never announces how many results were found, leaving users unaware of the outcome of their query.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation overlay lacks `aria‑hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the overlay element `_overlay` is hidden with the `hidden` attribute but never receives `aria-hidden`, so screen readers may still expose the overlay when it is not visible.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter empty‑state element is hidden without `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter._updateEmptyState()` adds the `hidden` attribute to `_emptyState` but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`, which can cause the element to remain in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Product‑count badge lacks an ARIA live region for dynamic updates
issuesFound.explanation: The element `_countBadge` displays the number of visible products, but it does not have `aria-live` or `aria-atomic`, so changes are not announced to screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu close animation does not clear `aria‑hidden` when the panel is reopened
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is reopened after being closed, the panel still carries the stale `aria-hidden="true"` attribute because the open routine never removes it, causing the panel to remain hidden to AT even though it is visible.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open handler does not set aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state lacks aria-hidden

issuesFound.fix: Line 720:   setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer shown state lacks removal of aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` method of `mobileNav` makes the drawer visible but does not remove a possible `aria-hidden` attribute, leaving the drawer hidden from assistive technologies even when displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 698:   removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');

issuesFound.title: Validation functions do not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` utility returns validation results but the calling code (e.g., `inlineValidator.validate`) never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on fields that fail validation, preventing screen readers from announcing errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3325:   if (!result.valid) setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); else removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: Validation functions do not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid after previously being invalid, `aria-invalid` is not removed, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3325:   if (result.valid) removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update in product filter not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters` function updates the product count badge and empty‑state message but does not write to a live‑region, so users are not informed when the list of products changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2740:   liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products shown`);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown does not trigger any live‑region announcement, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1152:   liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');
    Line 1170:   liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: While the FAQ open function announces the expanded question, the close function does not announce that the answer has collapsed, causing a lack of feedback for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2135:   liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed || `${item.question} — collapsed`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle switch does not set aria-checked on activation
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing toggle (`_onToggleClick`) changes the visual state but does not update `aria-checked`, leaving the switch’s state ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1620:   setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close does not update aria-controls on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the drawer, which is required for screen‑reader users to understand the relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Line 698:   setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close does not set aria-expanded on trigger (initial load)
issuesFound.explanation: Although `open` and `close` set `aria-expanded`, the initial HTML does not have `aria-expanded` defined, violating the requirement that the attribute be present on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 698:   setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // add during init if missing

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria-controls on init
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button does not have an `aria-controls` attribute linking it to its panel, which is required for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance` the panel is shown and ARIA attributes are set on the trigger for `aria-haspopup`, but the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `"true"` when the dropdown opens. This leaves screen‑reader users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item)` function in the `faqAccordion` module expands the answer panel and animates it, but it never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute to `"true"`. The button remains announced as collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening a FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chips UI updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `activeFilterChips.render()` function adds or removes chip elements to reflect the current product filters, but no live‑region announcement is made. Users of assistive technology are not informed when filters change.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering the chips, call `liveRegion.announce('Filters updated');` or a more specific message describing the added/removed filter.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input does not receive `aria‑invalid` on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form uses `inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required','email'])`, but the `inlineValidator` only adds error messages and CSS classes; it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input when validation fails. This prevents screen readers from detecting the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator.validate`, after a failed validation, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. Ensure the attribute is removed on successful validation.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Full name” field does not receive `aria‑invalid` on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The “Full name” input (`#contact-first-name` / `#contact-last-name` combined) is validated via `inlineValidator`, but the validator does not set `aria-invalid="true"` when the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Modify `inlineValidator.validate` to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field when validation fails.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Email address” field does not receive `aria‑invalid` on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 6 for the email input (`#contact-email`). No `aria-invalid` is set on error.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 6.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Subject” field does not receive `aria‑invalid` on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input (`#contact-subject`) is validated but never receives `aria-invalid="true"` on error.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 6.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Message” textarea does not receive `aria‑invalid` on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea (`#contact-message`) is validated but the validator does not set `aria-invalid`.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 6.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden only by removing CSS classes, not by `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: The `close()` function of `mobileNav` removes the open class from the drawer but never adds `aria-hidden="true"` (or removes it when opened). If the drawer is hidden via CSS transforms rather than `display:none`, assistive technologies may still expose it.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in `open()` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened or closed, the code emits custom events but does not provide an `aria-live` announcement. Users of screen readers are not informed that a menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region (aside from the existing announcement) – the close action lacks an announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `close(item)` function announces the opening of an item but does not announce when an item is collapsed, leaving users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `liveRegion.announce('Answer collapsed');` in the `close` function.  

These issues satisfy all mandatory JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) based on the Phase 1 inventory of the file.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard submit does not announce successful submission (MEDIUM)

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items do not update aria‑expanded on their question buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion toggles panels via class changes and `hidden` attributes, but the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the trigger button is never updated, leaving the state ambiguous for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open(item)` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `close(item)` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The validation system adds error messages and `aria‑describedby`, but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the input that failed validation. ATs will not announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside `markInvalid(field, errorEl)` add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`. Ensure the corresponding “valid” path removes the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Validation “valid” state does not clear aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid the script removes the error description but does not clear `aria‑invalid`. The field may remain announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
In `markValid()` add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Filter‑tab buttons lack aria‑pressed to indicate selection
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tabs use `CSS.active` class to show the selected tab, but they never set `aria‑pressed` (or `aria‑selected`) on the buttons, so assistive technologies cannot tell which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside the loop, after toggling the active class, also call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are hidden with `hidden` but not with `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed the script sets the `hidden` attribute but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"`. Some ATs rely on `aria‑hidden` for hidden state.
issuesFound.fix:
After `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` also call `inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`. Conversely, when opening, remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden with CSS classes only, not with `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden by removing the open class, but the script never adds `aria‑hidden="true"` to the drawer element, so ATs may still expose its content.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close()`, after removing the open class, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. In `open()`, remove that attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Live‑region announcements are missing for dynamic result‑count updates in the search module
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates a status `<div>` with text, but the element does not have `role="status"` or `aria‑live` attributes, so changes are not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure `_statusEl` is created with `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` (or reuse the existing element and set those attributes). Then update its `textContent` as done.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button (`data-billing-toggle`) does not expose its state via `aria‑pressed` (uses `aria‑checked` instead)
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle is a switch (`role="switch"`), but the script only updates `aria‑checked`. For a button that behaves like a toggle, `aria‑pressed` is the appropriate state indicator and is expected by many ATs.
issuesFound.fix:
In addition to `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', ...)`, also call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear));`.

---

issuesFound.title: Carousel navigation buttons lack `aria‑controls` linking them to the slide list
issuesFound.explanation: The “previous” and “next” buttons control the slide track but do not have `aria-controls` pointing to the list element, making it unclear which region they affect.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating `_prevBtn` and `_nextBtn`, add `setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-controls', _trackEl.id);` and similarly for `_nextBtn`. Ensure the track has an `id`.

---

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone does not expose its purpose via `aria‑label` when the default label is missing
issuesFound.explanation: If the developer does not provide a `data-upload-label`, the zone receives a generic label but may be empty, leaving screen‑reader users without context.
issuesFound.fix:
Provide a fallback label such as `setAttr(_zoneEl, 'aria-label', 'File upload – drag files here or click to select');` when no custom label is supplied.

---

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle buttons do not have `aria‑pressed` reflecting the current theme
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons change the theme but never update `aria‑pressed`, so AT users cannot know which theme is active.
issuesFound.fix:
After calling `set(_current)`, also call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === _current));`.

---

issuesFound.title: Sticky header adds `is-scrolled` class but does not set `aria‑hidden` on the header when it is hidden on scroll‑down
issuesFound.explanation: When the header hides, it is still perceivable to ATs because no `aria‑hidden` is applied.
issuesFound.fix: When adding the hidden class, also call `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and remove it when showing.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden with CSS classes only, not with `aria‑hidden`
File‑upload zone does not expose its purpose via `aria‑label` when the default label is missing
Theme‑switcher toggle buttons do not have `aria‑pressed` reflecting the current theme
Sticky header adds `is-scrolled` class but does not set `aria‑hidden` on the header when it is hidden on scroll‑down

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module’s open(), close() and toggle() functions change the visual state of the navigation drawer but never set or update aria‑expanded on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true') in open() and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false') in close(); toggle() can rely on those calls.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger element (`_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle)`) controls the drawer (`_drawer`) but no aria‑controls attribute is added, so assistive technologies cannot associate the button with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: After locating _trigger and _ drawer, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav-drawer'))`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden without aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but never sets aria‑hidden="true". The drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible, causing confusion for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In close(), after hiding the drawer, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in open() remove that attribute (`removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden')`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick and _onTriggerKeydown open and close dropdown panels by adding/removing CSS classes, but they never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving the expanded state undisclosed to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance() add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in _closeInstance() add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels hidden without aria‑hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance() sets panel.setAttribute('hidden', '') but does not set aria‑hidden="true". The panel stays in the accessibility tree while hidden.
issuesFound.fix: After adding the hidden attribute, also call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when opening, remove the attribute (`removeAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden')`).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels hidden without aria‑hidden when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close() adds hidden to the panel but does not set aria‑hidden="true", leaving the hidden content in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: After panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''), also call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when opening, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: newsletterForm binds the email input to inlineValidator, which displays an error message but never adds aria‑invalid="true" to the input when the value is invalid. Assistive technologies therefore do not announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the validation rule for the email field, after a failed validateField call, add `setAttr(emailInput, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; on successful validation, remove the attribute (`removeAttr(emailInput, 'aria-invalid')`).

issuesFound.title: Contact form fields validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: contactForm binds each field to inlineValidator, which marks the field with CSS classes but never sets aria‑invalid when validation fails, so screen‑reader users are not informed of the error.
issuesFound.fix: Extend inlineValidator.validate to call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` when result.valid is false, and `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')` when true.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown changes visible content, but the code only emits custom events (`dropdown:open`, `dropdown:close`) and does not write to an aria‑live region. Users who rely on screen readers receive no audible cue that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After opening a panel, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')`; after closing, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ item changes visible content, but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: In open(item) add `liveRegion.announce('Answer expanded')`; in close(item) add `liveRegion.announce('Answer collapsed')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions are not announced via a live region (already announced in code, but the announcement occurs after a setTimeout of 100 ms; this is acceptable, but the live region message is generic. No additional issue needed.)

fpTitles: Dropdown menu panels hidden without aria‑hidden attribute
Contact form fields validation does not set aria‑invalid on error

Mobile navigation open/close actions are not announced via a live region (already announced in code, but the announcement occurs after a setTimeout of 100 ms; this is acceptable, but the live region message is generic. No additional issue needed.)

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle` function changes the visibility of the navigation drawer but never updates the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button, leaving screen readers unaware of the menu’s state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(!_open));` after toggling the drawer’s open/closed state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) does not have an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, violating the requirement that toggle widgets expose their state on load.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init`, after locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation drawer does not provide an ARIA live announcement, so users are not informed of the change.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.openMenu || 'Navigation menu opened');` and in `close()` call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.closeMenu || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The click handler `_onTriggerClick` toggles a dropdown but never updates `aria-expanded` on the button, breaking accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: After opening or closing a dropdown, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(inst.open));` inside `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Users are not notified when a dropdown opens or closes because no ARIA live update occurs.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` and in `_closeInstance` call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item does not produce a live‑region announcement, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) || 'Answer expanded');` and in `close()` call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) || 'Answer collapsed');`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form uses `inlineValidator` to validate the email field but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on error, so screen readers cannot detect the problem.
issuesFound.fix: After a validation failure, add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and on success remove the attribute with `removeAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error (contact form name)
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form’s name field is validated via `inlineValidator` but does not receive `aria-invalid="true"` when invalid, preventing assistive technology from announcing the error.
issuesFound.fix: In `contactForm._onSubmit`, after detecting a validation error on the name field, call `setAttr(nameField, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and clear it on success with `removeAttr(nameField, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button controls the navigation drawer but lacks an `aria-controls` attribute that references the drawer’s `id`, breaking the relationship for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init`, ensure the drawer has an `id` (e.g., `if (!_drawer.id) _drawer.id = 'nav-drawer';`) and then add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);`.

fpTitles: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error (contact form name)

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function shows the mobile navigation drawer but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` adds the “open” class to a dropdown panel but never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button when closed
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance()` hides the dropdown panel without resetting `aria‑expanded` on the trigger, leaving the state incorrect for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Search input does not set aria‑expanded when suggestion panel opens
issuesFound.explanation: `_open()` in the site‑search module makes the suggestion list visible but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the search `<input>`. Users cannot tell whether the list is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening and `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Search input does not reset aria‑expanded when suggestion panel closes
issuesFound.explanation: `_close()` hides the suggestion list without clearing `aria‑expanded` on the `<input>`, leaving the attribute stale.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the close routine.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not set aria‑expanded on question button when opened
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item)` function expands an FAQ answer but never updates `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button, violating the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not reset aria‑expanded on question button when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `close(item)` function collapses the answer without resetting `aria‑expanded` on the trigger, leaving the state inaccurate for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger button missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is created without an `aria‑expanded` attribute, so its collapsed state is not conveyed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Initialise with `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger elements are given `aria-haspopup` but no initial `aria‑expanded="false"` value, so their collapsed state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` during registration.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` has `aria-controls` but no `aria-expanded` set to “false” on load, so its collapsed state is ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix: Initialise with `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ question button missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers receive `aria-controls` but lack an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, preventing screen readers from knowing they are collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when parsing each FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: Contact‑form first‑name field does not receive aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The `#contact-first-name` input is validated, but the validation routine only adds an error message element; it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on error, so AT cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the validation result handling, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` for invalid fields.

issuesFound.title: Contact‑form last‑name field does not receive aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 13 for `#contact-last-name`.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 13.

issuesFound.title: Contact‑form email field does not receive aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The email input is validated but never marked with `aria-invalid` when invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 13.

issuesFound.title: Contact‑form company field does not receive aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: Validation errors are not reflected via `aria-invalid`.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 13.

issuesFound.title: Contact‑form team‑size field does not receive aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: Missing `aria-invalid` on error.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 13.

issuesFound.title: Contact‑form message textarea does not receive aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: Validation errors are not reflected via `aria-invalid`.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 13.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter‑form email input does not receive aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email field is validated but never receives `aria-invalid="true"` on error, so screen readers cannot detect the problem.
issuesFound.fix: In the validation handling, set `aria-invalid` on the input when the email is invalid.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter updates product‑count badge without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters change, the visible count is updated but no `aria-live` region announces the new total, leaving users of assistive technology unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: Add an off‑screen live region (e.g., `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">`) and update its text with the new count each time the badge changes.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown changes visible content but no `aria-live` region is updated, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` or `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ answer changes the page content without a live‑region announcement, making the change invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Answer expanded')`; after closing, announce ‘Answer collapsed’.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list updates are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the suggestion panel opens or closes, no live‑region message is sent, so users are not informed that suggestions are available or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Use `liveRegion.announce('Search suggestions shown')` on open and `liveRegion.announce('Search suggestions hidden')` on close.  

These issues satisfy all required JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and meet the reporting format.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle function does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onTriggerClick` handler toggles the dropdown panel’s visibility but never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the panel state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu keyboard handler does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onTriggerKeydown` function handles opening/closing via keyboard but also fails to set `aria-expanded` on the trigger element.
issuesFound.fix: After opening the panel, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; after closing, set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: Period toggle buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncToggleState` function updates CSS classes for period buttons but never sets `aria-pressed` to reflect the active state, violating ARIA button state guidelines.
issuesFound.fix: In the loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive))` for each period button.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation hamburger button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle` function changes the drawer’s open state but does not update `aria-pressed` on the hamburger trigger, so assistive tech cannot tell whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')`; after closing, set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate` routine marks fields as invalid visually but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the input, so screen readers do not announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult`, when `!result.valid`, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; on success, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid on success
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the validator removes visual error classes but does not remove `aria-invalid`, leaving the invalid state announced incorrectly.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult`, when `result.valid`, call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers lack initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: During `dropdownMenu.init`, trigger buttons are given `aria-haspopup` but no initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so the initial collapsed state is not conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In the registration loop, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown close does not set aria‑hidden on hidden panel
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function hides the panel by removing a CSS class and setting `hidden`, but does not add `aria-hidden="true"`. Elements hidden with CSS may still be exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After hiding, call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when opening, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters` changes the visible product count and empty‑state message but never announces these changes to a live region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the result of their filter action.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count badge, call `liveRegion.announce(`${visibleCount} products shown`)`; similarly announce when no results are found.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not announce state
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` and `close` functions animate panels but do not use a live region to announce “question expanded” or “question collapsed”, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer expanded`)`; after closing, announce `${item.question} – answer collapsed`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce change
issuesFound.explanation: When the user selects a different billing period, the UI updates prices but no live‑region message is sent, so screen‑reader users cannot perceive the change.
issuesFound.fix: After `setPeriod`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Pricing switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`)`.

issuesFound.title: User actions (e.g., product filter sort change) lack dedicated live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the sort order via the `<select>` updates the product list but does not write to a dedicated live region; the generic status element is not used for this specific change.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onSortChange`, after `applyFilters()`, call `liveRegion.announce(`Sorted by ${sortKey} ${sortDir}`)`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu keyboard navigation does not announce panel open/close
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened via keyboard (Enter/Space), no live‑region message is emitted, so users cannot know the menu opened.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, add `liveRegion.announce(`${trigger.textContent.trim()} menu opened`)`; after closing, announce it closed.

fpTitles: User actions (e.g., product filter sort change) lack dedicated live‑region announcements

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm TextInput aria‑describedby points to a non‑existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The email <TextInput> in NewsletterForm sets aria-describedby to “newsletter-email-error” when there is an error, but no element with that id is ever rendered, so screen readers cannot locate the error message. This blocks users from understanding validation feedback.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm Name input aria‑describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Full name” sets aria-describedby to “contact-name-error”, but no element with that id exists, so error messages are not announced.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm Email input aria‑describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The email <TextInput> sets aria-describedby to “contact-email-error”, but that id is never rendered, preventing error announcement.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm Subject input aria‑describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The subject <TextInput> uses aria-describedby="contact-subject-error", but no such element exists, so validation feedback is invisible to AT.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm Message textarea aria‑describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The message <Textarea> sets aria-describedby to “contact-message-error”, but that id is never rendered, so screen readers cannot convey the error.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit <Button> does not communicate loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> receives a loading prop (which the component does not recognise) instead of isLoading, so aria‑busy is never set. Assistive technologies cannot tell that the button is busy.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm submit <Button> does not communicate loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> is passed a loading prop instead of isLoading, so aria‑busy is omitted and AT users cannot detect the in‑progress state.
issuesFound.fix:
Change the prop to isLoading.  
  Fix:  
  ```tsx
  // Line 1192
  <Button
    type="submit"
    variant="primary"
    size="md"
    isLoading={isSubmitting}
    aria-disabled={isSubmitting}
  >
    {isSubmitting ? 'Sending…' : 'Send message'}
  </Button>
  ```

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay <div> with onClick lacks button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The outer <div className="modal-overlay"> handles click to close the dialog but has no role, tabIndex, or keyboard handler, making it inaccessible to keyboard‑only users.
issuesFound.fix:
Replace the <div> with a <button> (or add role="button", tabIndex="0", onKeyDown) to expose the action.  
  Fix:  
  ```tsx
  // Line 1325
  <button
    type="button"
    className="modal-overlay"
    aria-modal="true"
    onClick={onClose}
    aria-label="Close dialog"
  >
    {/* inner dialog remains unchanged */}
  </button>
  ```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop <div> with onClick lacks button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop"> is clickable to close the mobile menu but is not focusable nor announced as a control, preventing keyboard users from dismissing the panel.
issuesFound.fix:
Change the element to a <button> (or add appropriate ARIA role and keyboard handling).  
  Fix:  
  ```tsx
  // Line 720
  <button
    type="button"
    className="mobile-nav-backdrop"
    aria-hidden="true"
    onClick={onClose}
    aria-label="Close mobile navigation"
  />
  ```

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm TextInput aria-describedby points to non‑existent ID

issuesFound.fix:
Remove the aria-describedby prop (or point it to an existing element).  
  Line 822: `<TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required aria-required="true" invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} />`

issuesFound.title: Invalid description list structure breaks accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The <dl> element (line 519) contains a <div> wrapper (line 521) as a direct child, which violates the HTML content model for description lists. This structure can prevent assistive technologies from correctly associating the <dt> (question) and <dd> (answer) elements, degrading the experience for screen reader users navigating the FAQ section.
issuesFound.fix: Line 521: Remove the <div class="faq-item"> wrapper and apply classes directly to <dt> and <dd> if styling is needed, or restructure using <div> outside the <dl>.

issuesFound.title: Redundant ARIA role on header element

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer panel missing aria-hidden toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (_drawer) visibility is toggled via CSS class (is-open), but the JavaScript does not update aria-hidden on the drawer element itself. If the CSS uses transform for off-canvas behaviour rather than display:none, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree when closed, confusing screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1648: setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');
  Line 1678: setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button lacks keyboard event handler
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button (_btn) has a click listener attached in init(), but no keydown listener is added. If the HTML element is not a native <button> or <a>, keyboard users cannot activate it with Enter or Space.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1966: _btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _onClick(); } });

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher toggle lacks keyboard event handler
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher listens for click events on [data-theme-toggle] elements but does not attach a keydown listener. If these elements are not native buttons, keyboard users cannot toggle the theme.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3377: document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { const btn = closest(e.target, '[data-theme-toggle]'); if (btn && (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE)) { e.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } });

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button aria-pressed not initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button's aria-pressed state is only set inside play() and pause() functions. If autoPlay is configured as false, neither function runs on init, leaving aria-pressed uninitialised on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3053: setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing));

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel navigation buttons lack keyboard enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: Click listeners are attached to _prevBtn, _nextBtn, and _dotsEl without verifying they are native buttons or adding keydown handlers. Non-native elements will not be keyboard accessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3032: [_prevBtn, _nextBtn, _playBtn, ..._dotsEl.children].forEach(btn => { if (btn && btn.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { btn.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); btn.setAttribute('role', 'button'); btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } }); } });

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear button lacks keyboard event handler

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close state changes are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu` module's `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions update the visible state of the menu and the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger, but they do not announce this state change to screen reader users via a live region. While keyboard navigation moves focus (which is announced), mouse/touch clicks leave focus on the trigger, leaving users unaware the menu opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel auto-play toggle state is not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel` module's `play` and `pause` functions update the `aria-pressed` state and `aria-label` of the play/pause button, but they do not explicitly announce the change in auto-play status (e.g., "Auto-play paused") to screen readers. Relying solely on label changes is less robust than a live region announcement for dynamic state changes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function toggles the navigation drawer visibility but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close` function correctly sets it to "false", but the "true" state is never applied dynamically, leaving screen readers unaware that the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module toggles the `_drawer` element's visibility using CSS classes (`is-open`) but does not toggle the `aria-hidden` attribute. If the CSS hides the drawer using techniques other than `display: none` (e.g. transform), it remains in the accessibility tree when closed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `_closeInstance` function sets it to "false", causing the state to be permanently "false" after the first close, misleading assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu` module opens and closes navigation panels without announcing the state change to screen readers via a live region. While `mobileNav` announces its state, `dropdownMenu` relies solely on `aria-expanded` which may not be sufficient for all users in this context.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function expands the answer panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger button. The `close` function sets it to "false", resulting in an incorrect persistent "false" state for expanded items.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner show action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `cookieConsent._showBanner` function displays a dialog and traps focus but does not announce the banner's appearance to screen readers via a live region. Users may be confused by the sudden focus trap without context.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher click handler lacks keyboard support for non-button elements
issuesFound.explanation: The `themeSwitcher` module attaches a click listener to `[data-theme-toggle]` elements without verifying they are native buttons. If a `div` or `span` is used, it will not be accessible via keyboard (Enter/Space) as no keydown handler is registered.

fpTitles: mobileNav drawer visibility not synced with aria-hidden
themeSwitcher click handler lacks keyboard support for non-button elements

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds classes and emits events but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1653: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function missing aria-expanded and live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger, nor does it announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. This blocks access for users relying on ARIA states and auditory feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1839: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 1842: liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function correctly updates aria-expanded="false" but does not announce the closing action to screen readers. Users are not informed when the menu disappears, causing disorientation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1865: liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel and announces the change but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question button. The close function sets it to "false", leaving the state inconsistent for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2740: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid without this explicit state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1338: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

Line 3020: liveRegion.announce('Automatic slide rotation started');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. The `markValid` function removes `aria-invalid`, meaning the attribute is never asserted when an error actually exists, preventing assistive technology from identifying the invalid state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger elements missing aria-expanded state update
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not have their aria-expanded attribute updated when the dropdown panel opens or closes. The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions manage the panel's hidden attribute but fail to update the trigger's ARIA state, leaving assistive technology users unaware of the dropdown status.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1635: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 1650: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger element missing aria-expanded state update on open
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion item trigger button does not have its aria-expanded attribute set to "true" when the answer panel is opened. While the close() function correctly sets it to "false", the open() function omits this update, causing inconsistent state reporting for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2430: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded state toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element acts as a combobox trigger but does not toggle its aria-expanded attribute when the suggestion list opens or closes. The _open() and _close() functions manage the list visibility but fail to update the input's ARIA state, violating the combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2065: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 2075: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an error description via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. This prevents assistive technologies from identifying the field as having an error state, relying solely on the description which may not be sufficient.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2965: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. Fields that were previously invalid may retain the aria-invalid="true" state in the accessibility tree even after correction, confusing screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2980: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger element aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialised to "false" in the init() function. If the menu is closed on load (default), the trigger should explicitly declare this state to ensure accurate initial reporting to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3355: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger elements aria-expanded not initialised at registration
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is registered, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not initialised to "false". The panel is hidden, but the trigger lacks the corresponding ARIA state, leading to potential inconsistency before the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1595: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input aria-expanded not initialised at mount
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element is configured as a combobox (role="combobox", aria-haspopup="listbox") but its aria-expanded attribute is not initialised to "false" in the suggestionListRenderer.mount function. This leaves the initial state undefined for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1995: setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge function updates the text content and aria-label of the product count badge dynamically, but there is no aria-live region associated with this change. Screen reader users are not automatically notified when the product count changes after filtering.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2230: _countBadge.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');

issuesFound.title: pricing plan card price updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _updatePlanCard function updates the price display text when the billing period changes, but does not announce this change via an aria-live region. Users relying on screen readers may not be aware that the prices have been updated to reflect the new billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2715: plan.priceDisplay.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');

issuesFound.title: Form field aria-describedby references non-existent error ID
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for the newsletter email sets aria-describedby to 'newsletter-email-error' when validation fails. However, the parent FormGroup component expects errorId and errorMessage props to render the ErrorMessage, but receives an error prop instead. Consequently, the element with id 'newsletter-email-error' is never rendered, leaving screen readers with a broken reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1635: <TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required aria-required="true" invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} /> (Update FormGroup usage to pass errorId="newsletter-email-error" and errorMessage={email.state.error} instead of error prop)

fpTitles: Form field aria-describedby references non-existent error ID

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submission handler crashes preventing status announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The handleSubmit function calls email.reset() upon success, but the useField hook does not return a reset method. This causes a runtime error that prevents the subsequent announce() call from executing, so the success message is not announced to screen readers (WCAG 4.1.3).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1359: export function useField(...): { state: FieldState; onChange: ...; onBlur: ...; validate: ...; reset: () => void } { ... const reset = useCallback(() => setState({ value: initialValue, error: undefined, touched: false }), [initialValue]); return { state, onChange, onBlur, validate, reset }; }
  Line 1397: email.reset();

issuesFound.title: ContactForm submission handler crashes preventing status announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The handleSubmit function calls f.reset() on all fields upon success, but the useField hook does not return a reset method. This causes a runtime error that prevents the subsequent announce() call from executing, so the success message is not announced to screen readers (WCAG 4.1.3).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1465: export function useField(...): { state: FieldState; onChange: ...; onBlur: ...; validate: ...; reset: () => void } { ... const reset = useCallback(() => setState({ value: initialValue, error: undefined, touched: false }), [initialValue]); return { state, onChange, onBlur, validate, reset }; }
  Line 1471: allFields.forEach((f) => f.reset());

issuesFound.title: LuminaryPage Modal fails to restore focus to trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The Modal's onClose handler (closeDemoModal) attempts to focus demoTriggerRef.current. However, this ref is passed to SiteHeader which does not accept or attach it to any DOM element. Focus is lost or returns to body instead of the trigger button, violating focus management requirements (WCAG 3.2.1).

issuesFound.title: Global :focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none paired only with outline-offset, which produces no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot perceive which interactive element currently has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Forced-colors mode :focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, :focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator in Windows High Contrast Mode. Users relying on forced colors cannot see focus.

issuesFound.title: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The selector inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset to -3px, resulting in no visible focus indicator for elements using the focus-ring-inset class.

issuesFound.title: .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline-color but outline remains none from the base rule, producing no visible focus ring. The outline-color property has no effect when outline style is none.

issuesFound.title: .btn:focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Button focus states set outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus ring. Keyboard users cannot identify focused buttons.

issuesFound.title: .nav-toggle:focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the outline in focus-visible state with only outline-offset, leaving no visible indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Submenu link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus ring. Keyboard users navigating submenus cannot see which link is focused.

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons are frequently used and must meet minimum size requirements.

issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, resulting in a 28px touch target. While above 24px, it falls below the 44px recommended size without compensating padding.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Checkboxes are critical form controls that must be easily activatable.

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and width: auto, making screen-reader text visible to all users instead of hiding it visually. This breaks the intended accessible hiding pattern.
