issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border in the same rule block. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see any focus indicator on elements that match this selector, violating WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none. In forced-colors mode, box-shadow is suppressed by the OS, so outline is the only reliable focus indicator. Removing it leaves keyboard users with no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button’s focus-visible state removes the default outline with outline: none and provides only an outline-offset. Without a box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline, the element shows no visible focus indicator when focused via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu link focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. This removes the focus ring from dropdown menu links without providing a visible alternative, preventing keyboard users from tracking their focus position.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input’s focus-visible state sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0 without providing a box-shadow or border replacement in the same rule. Although the base :focus state has a box-shadow, this specific focus-visible override removes it entirely.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets an explicit min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small target.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 530:
  min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button class sets explicit min-width and min-height of 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with tremors or limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px (within the 24–43px range) but has zero padding. Without at least 8px of padding on each side, the effective touch target may not meet the recommended 44px size and could be difficult to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width, height, and min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the checkbox difficult to tap for users with motor disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility does not hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which keeps the content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually. Content intended only for screen readers will be displayed to all users, breaking the visual design and potentially confusing sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and applies a transform: translateY(1px) on :active. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial movement, which can cause vestibular disorders for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition including transform and a hover state with transform: perspective(...) rotateY(...). The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for this selector is empty, so the animation is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01), but the existing prefers-reduced-motion media query only targets .pricing-card:hover, leaving the featured card’s animation active for users who requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link class includes a transition with transform (translateY). Users with vestibular disorders who enable reduced motion preferences will still see the slide-in animation because no override is provided.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button (.hero-ctas .btn-primary) defines a transition including transform and a hover state with transform: translateY(-1px). No prefers-reduced-motion override exists for this specific selector, violating user motion preferences.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, removing the default focus ring without providing a visible alternative such as a box-shadow or non-zero outline. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 338: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode

issuesFound.fix: Line 347-349: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 354: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 358: .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible. This implementation renders the content visible and potentially removes it from the accessibility tree in some browsers, breaking the intended screen-reader-only behavior.

issuesFound.explanation: The button component includes transform in its transition property, creating spatial movement on state changes. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on global anchor elements
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline on hover/focus, border-bottom, or font-weight change) for links in the default state. This causes links to be distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Deprecated aria-dropeffect attribute on file upload zone
issuesFound.explanation: The fileUploadField module sets aria-dropeffect to "none" and "copy" on the drop zone element. This attribute is deprecated in ARIA 1.2 and should no longer be used. Assistive technologies may not support it, causing inconsistent drag-and-drop feedback for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4473: Remove `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect', 'none');` and remove `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect', 'copy');` at Line 4484. Rely on visual cues and the existing aria-label and role="button" semantics to convey the drop target functionality.

issuesFound.title: aria-selected incorrectly applied to table header elements
issuesFound.explanation: The planComparisonHighlighter module sets aria-selected="true" on table header (th) elements to indicate the highlighted plan column. The aria-selected attribute is only valid on elements with specific roles such as gridcell, listbox option, row, tab, or treeitem. Standard table header elements do not support this attribute, causing screen readers to ignore the selection state or announce it incorrectly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4127: Remove `setAttr(th, 'aria-selected', 'true');` and the corresponding removal at Line 4130. Use a visually hidden text or aria-label on the column header to indicate it is the featured/recommended plan instead.

issuesFound.title: Conflicting ARIA states on pricing period toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module sets both aria-pressed and aria-selected on the same period toggle buttons. These attributes serve different purposes: aria-pressed indicates a toggle button state, while aria-selected indicates selection within a list (e.g., tabs). Using both simultaneously creates contradictory accessibility information for screen readers, which may announce the element incorrectly or fail to convey the active billing period reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3994: Remove `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` OR remove `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` at Line 3995. If the buttons function as tabs within a tablist, retain only aria-selected. If they function as toggle buttons, retain only aria-pressed. Ensure the roving tabindex pattern (createRovingTabindex) is aligned with the chosen ARIA pattern.

fpTitles: Deprecated aria-dropeffect attribute on file upload zone
aria-selected incorrectly applied to table header elements
Conflicting ARIA states on pricing period toggle buttons

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer acts as a modal dialog that traps focus and blocks interaction with the rest of the page, but the drawer element (`_drawer`) lacks `aria-modal="true"`. Screen reader users may not realize they are in a modal context and that the underlying page content is inert while the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1424: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true');` immediately after `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line 1449: Add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal');` immediately after `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: File upload progress not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: During file upload, the progress is visually indicated by updating a `<progress>` element and a status span, but there is no live region announcement of the upload progress, completion, or errors for screen reader users. Users with visual impairments are not informed when an upload starts, completes, or fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3693: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.uploadStarted || 'Upload started');` after `entry.status = 'uploading';`. Line 3700: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.uploadComplete || 'Upload complete');` after `entry.status = 'done';`. Line 3713: Add `liveRegion.announceAssertive(STRINGS.uploadError || 'Upload failed');` after `_uploadError(binding, entry, 'Network error during upload.');`.

issuesFound.title: Filter chip removal not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When a user clicks to remove an active filter chip, the chip is removed from the DOM and the filter is updated, but there is no announcement confirming which filter was removed. Screen reader users may not realize the filter has been removed or which specific filter was cleared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3370: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.filterRemoved || 'Removed filter: {label}').replace('{label}', label));` before the switch statement (where `label` is the chip label being removed).

issuesFound.title: Price animation values not announced during transition
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes and price animations are enabled (and reduced motion is not preferred), the price values update visually but the changing intermediate values are not announced to screen readers. While the period change is announced, users do not hear the price value updating and must wait for the animation to complete to hear the final price.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2593: Add `plan.priceDisplay.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');` before the `animateNumber` call, and remove it in the `onComplete` callback after the animation finishes (to prevent excessive announcements during the animation).

issuesFound.title: Toggle button in pricing missing aria-pressed initialization check
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncToggleState` function sets `aria-pressed` on period buttons, but it does not verify that the toggle switch element (`_toggleEl`) has `aria-checked` initialized if it uses `role="switch"`. If the HTML markup lacks this attribute, the switch state may not be correctly communicated on initial load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2634: Add a check `if (_toggleEl && !_toggleEl.getAttribute('aria-checked')) { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year')); }` at the beginning of the function.

issuesFound.title: Wizard step error focus management incomplete
issuesFound.explanation: When step validation fails, the code focuses the first invalid field, but if that field is inside a container that is not yet visible or is disabled, the focus may be lost or may not be clearly announced. Additionally, the error message is not explicitly associated with the field via `aria-describedby` at validation time.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3546: After `scrollIntoView(errors[0].field);`, add `srUtils.addDescribedBy(errors[0].field, errors[0].field.nextElementSibling);` assuming the error element is next to the field, or ensure `inlineValidator.validate` is called which handles this association.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing aria-activedescendant initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with `aria-controls` pointing to the suggestion listbox, but `aria-activedescendant` is not initialized to an empty state. While the code removes it when the list is hidden, ensuring it starts without the attribute when no suggestion is active improves compatibility with some screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2230: Add `_inputEl.removeAttribute('aria-activedescendant');` after setting up the input to ensure it starts clean.

issuesFound.title: Toast container missing aria-live management
issuesFound.explanation: Individual toasts have `aria-live`, but the container itself (`_containerEl`) is created with `aria-live="polite"`. Having aria-live on both the container and individual items may cause duplicate announcements in some screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1124: Remove `[ARIA.LIVE]: 'polite',` from the container creation, as the individual toasts already have `role="alert"` and `aria-live` attributes which are sufficient for announcements.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion search input missing aria-controls update
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ search input is given `aria-controls` pointing to the FAQ list container, but if the list container is dynamically replaced or significantly updated during filtering, the `aria-controls` reference may become stale. Additionally, the search input lacks `aria-autocomplete="list"` which is appropriate for this search-as-you-type pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3136: Add `setAttr(_searchInputEl, 'aria-autocomplete', 'list');` alongside the other aria attributes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds CSS classes to display the navigation drawer and locks scroll, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state change to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1243: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding _closeInstance correctly sets it to "false", leaving the attribute in an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1382: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function animates the panel height and updates the open property, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing that the accordion item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2103: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The validation utility marks fields as invalid by linking the error message via aria-describedby, but omits setting aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1752: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers. Users may be unaware that menu content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1385 (in _openInstance): Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);`
  Line 1397 (in _closeInstance): Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);`

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField zone missing keyboard activation handler
issuesFound.explanation: The upload zone is given role="button" and tabindex="0" but only handles click events. It lacks a keydown handler for Enter and Space keys, making it inaccessible to keyboard users who cannot activate the file input.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger potentially missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The code sets aria-controls only if the drawer already has an id. If the drawer element lacks an id, the aria-controls attribute is never set, creating a broken or missing reference for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing accessible name fallback
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is selected from the DOM but the code assumes it has an accessible name. If the HTML lacks an aria-label or text content, the button will have no accessible name for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The clear button is selected via SELECTORS.searchClear but may not have an aria-label or visible text. If the HTML provides neither, the button will be announced as "button" without a name.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard step panels missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: Step panels are assigned role="tabpanel" but are not explicitly labelled via aria-labelledby pointing to their respective headings. Screen readers may not announce the step title when focus moves into the panel.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button is created in the DOM but may not have aria-pressed set to reflect the initial auto-play state before user interaction.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle missing aria-checked initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch may not have aria-checked set initially if the HTML lacks it, even though the JavaScript manages the state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: While the close function sets aria-expanded="false" in the code path shown in the init function's cleanup, the standalone close function shown in the snippet does not explicitly set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger before animating.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2122: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `item.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds the error element to aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state of form inputs to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1 Error Identification. This affects all forms using inline validation (newsletter, contact, wizard).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1019 (inside markInvalid): Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); after the isElement checks.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav initialization fails to activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a _trap variable for focus management but never instantiates createFocusTrap() or activates it in the open() function. Keyboard users can tab out of the mobile navigation drawer into the background page, causing focus loss and disorientation, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1901 (inside open function): Add _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true }); _trap.activate(); before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() fails to return focus to trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer closes, the function deactivates the focus trap (if it existed) but does not programmatically return keyboard focus to the trigger button that opened it. Focus is lost to the document body, forcing keyboard users to tab from the beginning, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1935 (inside close function, before emit): Add if (_trigger) _trigger.focus(); after _unlockScroll() and before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds CSS classes to display the navigation drawer and locks scroll, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the menu when it opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert after "_open = true;": setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, the function sets the instance state to open and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen reader users from knowing the dropdown is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert after "inst.open = true;": setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function marks the FAQ item as open and animates the panel height, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce that the answer panel is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert after "item.open = true;": setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons indicating the active filter category, but the function only toggles CSS classes and does not set aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert after "toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);": setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function links the error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert after "addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);": field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus not trapped in drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the code does not activate a focus trap, allowing keyboard users to tab out of the drawer into the background page content. The _trap variable is also never initialized with createFocusTrap.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert after "_lockScroll();": if (!_trap) { _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); } _trap.activate(); const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();

issuesFound.title: characterCounter missing aria-describedby link
issuesFound.explanation: The character counter element is created with aria-live but is not linked to the input field via aria-describedby. Screen reader users will not hear the character count instructions or current count when focusing the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert after the insertBefore line: const counterId = srUtils.ensureId(counterEl, 'char-counter'); setAttr(field, 'aria-describedby', ((field.getAttribute('aria-describedby') || '') + ' ' + counterId).trim());

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility by adding/removing CSS classes and managing focus traps, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the open/closed state. Screen readers will not announce the state change to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1518: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);
  Line 1539: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in dropdownMenu show and hide dropdown panels but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger elements. While the register() function initializes aria-expanded to false, the toggle functions do not maintain this state, leaving screen readers unaware of the current expansion state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1680: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');
  Line 1696: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after if (!inst.open) inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands FAQ answer panels and updates CSS classes but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetry where the expanded state is never communicated to assistive technologies when opening an item.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2942: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function selects the trigger button and drawer elements but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger during page load. Since the drawer is closed by default, the trigger must communicate this state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1580: After _trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle); add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function adds error message references via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the control element. This helper is used throughout the validation system, causing all validation errors to lack the required ARIA invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1084: Add setAttr(control, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); after addDescribedBy(control, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() utility function removes error message references from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute from the control element. Valid fields may still be reported as invalid by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1095: Add setAttr(control, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); or removeAttr(control, 'aria-invalid'); after the existing aria-describedby cleanup logic;

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod does not announce billing change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function updates price displays and billing period UI when users toggle between monthly and annual billing, but it does not announce this change to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technologies will not be informed that prices have changed or what the new billing period is.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2650: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices switched to ' + period + ' billing'); after _syncToggleState(period);

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce filter results
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates the product grid visibility and count badges when filtering, but does not announce the filter results or count changes to screen readers. Users will not know that the filter has been applied or how many results are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2334: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied || 'Showing ' + totalMatches + ' products'); inside the filterAnimation.run callback after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches);

issuesFound.title: productFilter view mode toggle lacks keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter module uses delegate(document.body, '[data-view-mode]', 'click', _onViewToggle) to handle view mode toggles (grid/list), but does not ensure these elements are buttons or provide Enter/Space keyboard support. If the HTML uses div or span elements for these toggles, they will be inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2374: Ensure the delegate handler checks for keyboard support or add explicit keyboard handling: 
  Line 2374: Replace with code that checks if (ev.type === 'click' || ev.key === 'Enter' || ev.key === ' ') and ensure target elements have role="button" and tabindex="0", or use native button elements.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher theme toggle lacks keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher.init() function attaches a click listener to document that delegates to [data-theme-toggle] elements to switch themes, but does not ensure these elements are buttons or add Enter/Space keyboard handlers. If the HTML uses non-button elements (e.g., divs or spans) for theme toggles, keyboard users cannot activate them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3562: Add explicit keydown handling for Enter and Space keys, or ensure elements matching [data-theme-toggle] are native button elements with proper tabindex. Add check: if (ev.type === 'keydown' && (ev.key !== 'Enter' && ev.key !== ' ')) return; and ensure elements have role="button" and tabindex="0" if not already buttons.

issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, they are not initialised with aria-expanded="false". Although the panel is hidden, the trigger lacks the attribute to indicate its relationship to the collapsed panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line register (after panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')): Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element lacks aria-expanded="false" on page load. Since it controls a suggestion panel that is initially hidden, the input should declare its initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line init (after setting up input attributes): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled via the open() and close() functions, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2204: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside the open function after `_open = true;`. Line 2222: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside the close function after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions manage dropdown panel visibility but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. The attribute is only initialized in register() but never synchronized with the actual state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2370: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2382: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open and close functions manage panel visibility via animateHeight and hidden attributes, but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4120: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside open function after `item.open = true;`. Line 4137: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside close function after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons showing the active category, but the function only toggles CSS classes. It does not set aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3050: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual/quarterly) indicate the active period but only update CSS classes. They lack aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3550: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1880: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside markInvalid function after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validation renderer calls srUtils.markInvalid for invalid fields, but since markInvalid does not set aria-invalid, the attribute is never applied to form fields showing validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3320: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside the if (!result.valid) block before calling srUtils.markInvalid, and `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` inside the if (result.valid) block.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates and the grid changes, but no announcement is made via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the filter has been applied or how many results are showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2915: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(totalMatches) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);` inside the filterAnimation.run callback before emitting the event.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled (monthly/yearly), the prices update visually with animation, but the change is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the price change or the new billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3535: Add `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing\`);` after updating the plan cards.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens or closes, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2210: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` in open function. Line 2232: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` in close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, the state change is not announced to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2375: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` in _openInstance. Line 2390: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.explanation: The contact form first name input (selector '#contact-first-name') is a personal data field that should have autocomplete="given-name" to help users fill forms, but the initialization does not set this attribute.

issuesFound.explanation: The contact form last name input (selector '#contact-last-name') should have autocomplete="family-name" but the initialization does not set this attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4516: Add `if (_lastNameEl) _lastNameEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'family-name');` after querying the element.

issuesFound.explanation: The contact form email input (selector '#contact-email') should have autocomplete="email" but the initialization does not set this attribute.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialisation on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is closed by default when the page loads, but the trigger button is not programmatically given aria-expanded="false" during initialisation. Assistive technologies cannot infer the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line init(): after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls reference to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button controls the _drawer element, but no aria-controls attribute is set on the trigger pointing to the drawer's ID. Screen reader users cannot identify which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line init(): after `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav);`, add `if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing users to tab outside to background content. The _trap variable is declared but never instantiated or activated, violating WCAG 2.4.3 focus order requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line open(): after `_lockScroll();`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions omit aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons toggle the visibility of their associated panels, but the functions _openInstance and _closeInstance do not synchronise the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger to match the open or closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line _openInstance(): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line _closeInstance(): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown instances are registered during init, the trigger button lacks the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute even though the panel is hidden by default. This prevents screen readers from announcing the collapsed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line register(): after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded via the open() function, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true". Although the close() function sets it to "false", the asymmetric update leaves screen readers unaware when content is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line open(): add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes visible content, but no aria-live region is updated to announce the state change to screen reader users. The announceToSR parameter exists but is never used to trigger an announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line open(): add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question));` before emit. Line close(): add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question));` before emit.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons indicating the active category, but the function only toggles CSS classes and does not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect which tab is currently pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line _syncTabAriaStates(): inside forEach, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge text is updated, but there is no accompanying announcement via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed how many results match their selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line _updateCountBadge(): after `_countBadge.textContent = String(count);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period switch not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates price displays visually, but the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving users unaware the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line setPeriod(): after `_updatePlanCard(plan, period);` (inside the forEach), add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` after the loop completes.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line markInvalid(): add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, the function removes the error description but leaves aria-invalid="true" set (or doesn't explicitly set it to "false"), causing screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line markValid(): add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` at the end of the function (or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`).

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator relies on srUtils for aria-invalid management
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validator depends on srUtils.markInvalid and srUtils.markValid to manage accessibility states, but since those functions do not handle aria-invalid (Issues 13 and 14), validation errors are never reflected in the aria-invalid attribute as required by WCAG.
issuesFound.fix: Line _renderResult(): replace calls to `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl)` and `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl)` with explicit `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` and `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false')` respectively, or ensure srUtils functions are updated as per Issues 13-14.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField zone missing aria-live for status updates
issuesFound.explanation: While individual file rows have status text, the overall upload progress and completion status are not announced via an aria-live region on the zone's status element. The statusEl exists but is not configured with aria-live in the bind() function.
issuesFound.fix: Line bind(): after `const statusEl = ...`, add `setAttr(statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite');` and ensure `_uploadEntry` updates this statusEl on completion/error, or use the existing `liveRegion.announce` calls consistently (currently only called in _uploadError and on add/remove, but progress updates are silent).

inlineValidator relies on srUtils for aria-invalid management

issuesFound.fix: Line 1416: Change `error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}` to `errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}`

issuesFound.title: Contact form email field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby="contact-email-error" when in error state, but the FormGroup component is not passed the errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage element with that ID is never rendered. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1432: Change `error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` to `errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}`

issuesFound.title: Contact form subject field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby="contact-subject-error" when in error state, but the FormGroup component is not passed the errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage element with that ID is never rendered. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1448: Change `error={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}` to `errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}`

issuesFound.title: Contact form message field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea has aria-describedby including "contact-message-error" when in error state, but the FormGroup component is not passed the errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage element with that ID is never rendered. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1467: Change `error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}` to `errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop div uses onClick without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop div has an onClick handler to close the navigation but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers for Enter and Space. While it has aria-hidden="true", it is only reachable by mouse users, creating a discrepancy where keyboard users cannot click the backdrop to close (though Escape works).
issuesFound.fix: Line 956: Replace `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" aria-hidden="true" onClick={onClose} />` with `<button type="button" className="mobile-nav-backdrop" aria-hidden="true" tabIndex={-1} onClick={onClose} style={{position: 'absolute', inset: 0, background: 'transparent', border: 'none', padding: 0, margin: 0}} />` or add role="button" tabIndex={0} and onKeyDown handler.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div uses onClick without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div has onClick={onClose} to allow clicking outside to close, but it lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard handlers. While focus is trapped inside the dialog, the overlay is not keyboard accessible, creating a mouse-only interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1736: Add role="button" tabIndex={0} and onKeyDown handler, or change to a button element with appropriate styling.

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on incorrect element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element. According to ARIA specifications, aria-modal should be on the element with role="dialog" (the inner div at line 1740), not the outer overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1736: Remove aria-modal="true" from the overlay div. Line 1740: Add aria-modal="true" to the inner div with role="dialog".

issuesFound.title: Skip link to main navigation broken on mobile viewports
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a link to "#main-nav" (href="#main-nav"), but the DesktopNav component with id="main-nav" is only rendered when !isMobile. On mobile viewports, this element does not exist in the DOM, making the skip link target missing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 367: Ensure the link target exists on all viewports by adding id="main-nav" to the MobileNav navigation element or conditionally rendering the skip link based on viewport.

issuesFound.title: Skip link to search broken on mobile viewports
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a link to "#site-search", but the SearchInput form with id="site-search" is only rendered when !isMobile. On mobile viewports, this element does not exist, making the skip link target missing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 369: Ensure the search form is always rendered or conditionally render the skip link based on viewport.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not currently visible (isVisible === false) are not hidden from assistive technology with aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to read all slides instead of just the active one.

issuesFound.title: ComparisonTableSection table cells using invalid aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The td elements use aria-label to provide accessible text for checkmarks and dashes, but aria-label is not a valid attribute on td elements per HTML5 specification and is not reliably supported by assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div has onClick without keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div has an onClick handler to close the modal when clicking the backdrop, but it lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers for Enter and Space, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay incorrectly has aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element itself. The aria-modal attribute must be on the element with role="dialog" to properly indicate that content outside the dialog is inert.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid for error state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to apply error styling but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute, preventing screen readers from announcing the invalid state of the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 582: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the input element.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent disclosure trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ trigger button toggles the answer panel using the isOpen state but lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen reader users from knowing if the answer is currently expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop to apply error styling but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users when form validation fails.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references an error message ID via aria-describedby, but the ErrorMessage component is never rendered with that ID because FormGroup receives the wrong prop name (`error` instead of `errorMessage`) and lacks the `errorId` prop. Screen readers will announce a reference to non-existent content.
issuesFound.fix:
Update FormGroup usage in NewsletterForm:
    <FormGroup
      label="Email address"
      htmlFor="newsletter-email"
      errorId="newsletter-email-error"
      errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
    >
      <TextInput
        id="newsletter-email"
        type="email"
        name="email"
        placeholder="you@company.com"
        autoComplete="email"
        value={email.state.value}
        onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)}
        onBlur={email.onBlur}
        required
        invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}
        aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}
      />
    </FormGroup>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references an error message ID that does not exist in the DOM because FormGroup is passed `error` instead of `errorMessage` and no `errorId` is provided, preventing the ErrorMessage from rendering.
issuesFound.fix:
Update FormGroup usage for the name field:
    <FormGroup
      label="Full name"
      htmlFor="contact-name"
      required
      errorId="contact-name-error"
      errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}
    >
      <TextInput
        id="contact-name"
        name="name"
        type="text"
        autoComplete="name"
        required
        aria-required="true"
        value={name.state.value}
        onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)}
        onBlur={name.onBlur}
        invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error}
        aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined}
      />
    </FormGroup>

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger button missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ disclosure button toggles the visibility of its answer panel (referenced by aria-controls) but does not expose the expanded/collapsed state via aria-expanded. Screen-reader users are not informed when content is shown or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2086: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous button is an icon-only control (contains only the "‹" character) rendered via IconButton, but the required aria-label prop is missing. Screen-reader users hear only "button" with no context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2194: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel next button is an icon-only control (contains only the "›" character) rendered via IconButton, but the required aria-label prop is missing. Screen-reader users hear only "button" with no context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">›</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Contact form name field error message ID not rendered
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references 'contact-name-error' in aria-describedby when an error exists, but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage element is never rendered with that ID. This creates a broken aria-describedby reference for screen-reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2263-2268: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Contact form email field error message ID not rendered
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references 'contact-email-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup receives only the error prop (renamed to errorMessage internally) and not errorId, so the error message element lacks the expected ID. Assistive technology cannot find the referenced description.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2279-2284: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Contact form subject field error message ID not rendered
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references 'contact-subject-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup is missing the errorId prop, causing the ErrorMessage to render without an id attribute. This breaks the programmatic association between input and error text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2295-2300: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Contact form message field error message ID not rendered
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea references 'contact-message-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup does not receive the errorId prop, so the error message element is not created with the expected ID. Screen-reader users cannot access the error description.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2312-2317: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field error message ID not rendered
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup is not passed errorId, so the ErrorMessage is rendered without an id. This creates a dangling aria-describedby reference that points to a non-existent element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2233-2238: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to style error states but does not map it to aria-invalid. Screen-reader users are not notified when the field is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 788-795: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not set aria-invalid on the element. Assistive technology cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 804-809: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label or aria-labelledby. Since multiple nav elements exist on the page, screen-reader users cannot distinguish this skip-navigation landmark from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 643: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection uses a <section> element without aria-label or aria-labelledby. As one of multiple sections on the page, it lacks a unique accessible name for landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1324: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar uses a <section> element that contains a heading with id="stats-heading", but the section does not reference it via aria-labelledby. Screen-reader users cannot identify this landmark region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component renders navigation links that receive a visual current indicator (nav-link--current class) when item.current is true, but does not add aria-current="page". Screen-reader users are not informed which page is current.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1054: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The MobileNav component renders links with a visual current state (mobile-nav__link--current) but omits aria-current="page". Assistive technology cannot convey the current page location to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1144: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan card CTA missing unique accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard CTA link uses generic text like "Get started for free" without an aria-label that includes the plan name. When multiple pricing cards are present, screen-reader users cannot distinguish which plan each button refers to.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1945: <a href={plan.ctaHref} aria-label={`${plan.ctaLabel} — ${plan.name} plan`} className={['btn', plan.featured ? 'btn--primary' : 'btn--outline', 'btn--md'].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The MobileNav panel has role="dialog" and acts as a modal (blocking interaction with the rest of the page), but lacks aria-modal="true". Screen-reader users may not perceive the dialog as modal and could attempt to interact with background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: <div id={menuId} ref={panelRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label={`${SITE.name} navigation`} className={['mobile-nav', isOpen ? 'mobile-nav--open' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} hidden={!isOpen}>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop not keyboard accessible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile-nav-backdrop div has an onClick handler to close the menu but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard-only users cannot dismiss the menu by activating the backdrop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1094: <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" aria-hidden="true" role="button" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} />

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link indicating the current page uses a visual class 'nav-link--current' but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify which page is current, violating WCAG 2.4.8.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link indicating the current page uses a visual class 'mobile-nav__link--current' but lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from announcing the current location.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When the Button component is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it renders a spinner and disables the button but does not set aria-busy. Assistive technology users are not notified that the button is processing and the content is being updated, violating WCAG 4.1.3.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label or aria-labelledby. When multiple navigation landmarks exist on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish the purpose of this specific navigation block, violating WCAG 2.4.1 and 1.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 830: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component renders a <nav> element with an id but no aria-label. Without an accessible name, screen reader users cannot distinguish this primary navigation from other navigation landmarks (such as footer or mobile navigation), violating WCAG 1.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 893: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav panel navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The MobileNav component contains a <nav> element wrapping the navigation list, but it lacks an aria-label. This makes it indistinguishable from other navigation regions when using screen reader landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 952: <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection component renders a <section> element without aria-label or aria-labelledby. Although it contains a heading with id="hero-heading", the section is not programmatically associated with it, making the landmark harder to identify for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1125: <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar component renders a <section> element without aria-label or aria-labelledby. It contains a heading with id="stats-heading" that should be referenced to provide an accessible name for the landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1194: <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard component renders an <article> element without aria-labelledby. The article contains a heading with id={headingId}, but the article is not programmatically associated with it, making the card region unnamed for screen reader users navigating by article.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1577: <article className="pricing-card__article" aria-labelledby={headingId}>

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The IntegrationsBar component duplicates the integration list for a seamless visual marquee effect. The second (cloned) list is not marked with aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce the integration partners twice, violating WCAG 1.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1259: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: StarRating widget missing role and decorative icons not hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The StarRating component renders star characters (★/☆) inside spans without aria-hidden. The container lacks role="img", causing screen readers to read "black star" or "white star" for each icon rather than the intended rating value.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 835: <span role="img" aria-label={`${labelPrefix}: ${value} out of ${max} stars`}>
  Line 841: <span key={i} className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'} aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The required field indicator in FormGroup renders a bare asterisk character (*) without aria-hidden. Screen readers may announce this as "star" separately from the "(required)" text that follows, creating redundant and potentially confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 878: *
